channel.title: Books on Techiedeepdive
channel.description: Recent content in Books on Techiedeepdive

item.title: Reading List
item.description: 2020 Learn to earn - Peter Lynch Assertions and Functional coverage By Ashok Mehta Computer organization and design (Reread) FPGA Prototyping by Verilog Examples: Xilinx Spartan-3 Version. Advance ASIC chip synthesis by Himanshu Bhatnagar. SystemC: From the Ground Up by Black, D.C., Donovan, J., Bunton, B., Keist, A. Digital Design and Computer Architecture by David Harris and Sarah Harris. Open Verification Methodology Cookbook by Mark Glasser. Hardware Verification With Systemverilog By Mintz and Ekendahl.

item.title: Book review - The Pragmatic Programmer
item.description: The Pragmatic Programmer is a small book about the best practices to be a “Pragmatic” Programmer. It talks about handling software projects with large teams, changing requirements and complex environments. I liked the first chapter where it goes through the philosophy of pragmatic engineers. I have been following these practices for years but i haven’t see them actually written somewhere. things, like investing in personal portofolio, are the best investment an engineer can do.
